name: v05-csv-import
description: CSV-import pipeline with per-row resilience. Receives an array of rows in the body and processes each through validate → transform → insert. Each row is wrapped in its own tryCatch, so a single bad row doesn't abort the whole import — failed rows go to a per-row DLQ slot the response surfaces. Real deployments swap the inline `inserts` summary for an actual DLQ table or queue. Demonstrates: forEach (sequential — DB inserts shouldn't run wild) + per-iteration tryCatch with $.error.message into the DLQ payload + iter-summary expr that distinguishes ok from failed for the post-loop summary.

inputs.expression: ({ rowId: ctx.state.row.id || null, ok: ctx.state['insert-row'] !== undefined, error: ctx.state.rowError || null })

((arr) => { const summaries = arr.map(r => (r && r.data && typeof r.data === 'object') ? r.data : r); const ok = summaries.filter(s => s && s.ok === true); const failed = summaries.filter(s => s && s.ok === false); return { totalRows: summaries.length, inserted: ok.length, dlq: failed.map(f => ({ rowId: f.rowId, error: f.error })) }; })(ctx.state['process-rows'] || [])
